questions.q: The remainder when <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mfenced><mn>11</mn></mfenced><mn>1011</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><mfenced><mn>1011</mn></mfenced><mn>11</mn></msup></math> is divided by <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>9</mn></math> is _____ .
questions.solution:
111011=113327=9k+8327

when divided by 9 is equivalent to the division of 

8327=9-1327

i.e. remainder=-1+9=8

Now, 101111=9m+311

when divided by 9 is equivalent to the division of 

311=95×3

i.e. remainder=0

∴   111011+101111 has same remainder as 8.

questions.q:
If b is very small as compared to the value of a, so that the cube and other higher powers of ba can be neglected in the identity

1a-b+1a-2b+1a-3b+….+1a-nb=αn+βn2+γn3

then the value of γ is :
questions.solution:
Given,

1a-b+1a-2b+1a-3b+….+1a-nb

=a-b-1+a-2b-1+….+a-nb-1

=1a∑r=1n1-rba-1

=1a∑r=1n1+rba+r2b2a2+terms to be neglected

=1an+n(n+1)2·ba+n(n+1)(2n+1)6·b2a2

=1an+n2+n2·ba+2n3+3n2+n6·b2a2

So γ=b23a3

questions.q:
Among the statements :

(S1) : 20232022-19992022 is divisible by 8.

(S2) : 13(13)n-11n-13 is divisible by 144 for infinitely many n∈ℕ
questions.solution:
Given,

(S1) : (2023)2022-(1999)2022 is divisible by 8

Now we know that (x-y) divides xn-yn ∀n∈ℕ

So, 2023-1999 divides (2023)2022-(1999)2022

⇒ 24 divides (2023)2022-(1999)2002

⇒ 8 will divide (2023)2022-(1999)2002

As 8 divides 24

Hence, S1 is correct

Now solving,

S2 : 1313n-11n-13 is divisible by 144 for n∈ℕ

So using binomial theorem in 1+12n we get,

131+12n-11n-13

=13nC0+nC112+nC2122+...+nCn12n-11n-13

=12×13n-11n+122λ

=145n+144λ which is not divisible by 144

Hence, S2 is incorrect

questions.q: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>a</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>b</mi></math> be two real numbers such that <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>a</mi><mi>b</mi><mo><</mo><mn>0</mn></math>. If the complex number <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><mi>a</mi><mi>i</mi></mrow><mrow><mi>b</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>i</mi></mrow></mfrac></math> is of unit modulus and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>a</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>i</mi><mi>b</mi></math> lies on the circle <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced open="|" close="|"><mrow><mi>z</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mfenced open="|" close="|"><mrow><mn>2</mn><mi>z</mi></mrow></mfenced></math>, then a possible value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><mfenced close="]" open="["><mi>a</mi></mfenced></mrow><mrow><mn>4</mn><mi>b</mi></mrow></mfrac></math>, where <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced close="]" open="["><mi>t</mi></mfenced></math> is greatest integer function, is :
questions.solution:
Given,

a,b be two real numbers such that ab<0, 1+aib+i=1, a+ib lies on the circle z-1=2z and ab<0

Now using 1+aib+i=1 we get,

⇒1+ai=b+i

Now squaring both side we get,

⇒1+ai2=b+i2

⇒1+ai1-ai=b+ib-i z2=zz¯

⇒1+a2=b2+1

⇒a=±b⇒b=-a as ab<0

Now given,

a,b lies on z-1=2z

So, a+ib-1=2a+ib

⇒a-1+ib2=4a+ib2

⇒a-12+b2=4a2+b2

⇒a-12+a2=42a2 as b=-a

⇒6a2+2a-1=0

⇒a=-2±2812=-1±76

Taking positive sign we get,

a=7-16 &  b=1-76  as b=-a

Now the value of a=0 as 0<7-16<1

∴   1+a4b=641-7=-1+74

Now taking negative sign we get, a=-1-76 &  b=7+16

Now  a=-1 as -1<-1-76<0

So, the value of 1+a4b=  1+-14b=04b=0

Note- Official answer key was drop by NTA, here we have modified the option.

Then, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>p</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>q</mi><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>q</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>p</mi><mo>-</mo><mi>q</mi><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>q</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></math> are roots of the equation.
questions.solution:
Given:

(1-3i)200=2199(p+iq)

⇒12-32i200=12p+iq

⇒cosπ3-isinπ3200=12p+iq

⇒cos200π3-isin200π3=12p+iq

⇒cos201π-π3-isin201π-π3=12p+iq

⇒-cosπ3-isinπ3=12p+iq

⇒2-12-i32=p+iq

⇒-1-i3=p+iq

So,

p=-1, q=-3

α=p+q+q2=2-3

β=p-q+q2=2+3

So,

α+β=4

α·β=1

Required equation is

x2-4x+1=0

questions.q: Let z1=2+3i and z2=3+4i. The set S=z∈C:z-z12-z-z22=z1-z22 represents a
options: straight line with sum of its intercepts on the coordinate axes equals <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>14</mn></math>
hyperbola with the length of the transverse axis <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>7</mn></math>
straight line with the sum of its intercepts on the coordinate axes equals <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>-</mo><mn>18</mn></math>
questions.solution:
Given,

z1=2+3i and z2=3+4i and the set

S=z∈C:z-z12-z-z22=z1-z22,

Now putting the value of z=x+iy, z1=2+3i and z2=3+4i in z-z12-z-z22=z1-z22 we get,

⇒(x-2)2+(y-3)2-(x-3)2-(y-4)2=1+1

⇒   x+y=7

Which is a straight line with intercept 7 on x & y axis, so sum of intercept will be 7+7=14.

Then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>z</mi></math> lies on the circle of radius <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn></math> and centre
questions.solution:
Given equation is

z-2iz+i=2, z≠-i

On simplifying the equation, we get

(z-2i)(z¯+2i)=4(z+i)(z¯-i)

⇒zz¯+4+2i(z-z¯)=4(zz¯+1+i(z¯-z))

⇒3zz¯-6i(z-z¯)=0

Now putting the value of z=x+iy & z¯=x-iy we get,

⇒x2+y2-2i(2iy)=0

⇒x2+y2+4y=0

On comparing the above equation with the general equation of the circle i.e

(x-h)2+(y-k)2=r2

We get, centre (0, -2)

questions.q: Let a≠b be two non-zero real numbers. Then the number of elements in the set X=z∈C:Reaz2+bz=a and Rebz2+az=b is equal to
questions.solution:
Given,

X=z∈C:Reaz2+bz=a and Rebz2+az=b

Now, let z=x+iy

So,  Reaz2+bz=a

⇒Reax+iy2+bx+iy=a

⇒Reax2-y2+2ixy+bx+iy=a

⇒ax2-y2+bx=a    ... i

And,  Rebz2+az=b

⇒Rebx+iy2+ax+iy=b

⇒Rebx2-y2+2ixy+ax+iy=b

⇒ bx2-y2+ax=b   ... ii

From i and ii, i-ii we get,

x2-y2a-b-xa-b=a-b

⇒ x2-y2-x=1  ... iii

From i and ii, i+ii

x2-y2+x-1a+b=0 (Note: here a+b≠0 is considered but it is not clear from the question)

⇒x2-y2+x-1=0  ... iv

Now from iii and iv we get,

x=0, y2=-1 (No solution)

questions.q: Let C be the circle in the complex plane with centre z0=121+3i and radius r=1. Let z1=1+i and the complex number z2 be outside circle C such that z1-z0z2-z0=1. If z0, z1 and z2 are collinear, then the smaller value of z22 is equal to
questions.solution:
Given,

z0=1+3i2, z1=1+i

So, z1-z0=1-122+1-322=14+14=12

And z2 be outside circle, z1-z0z2-z0=1

⇒12z2-z0=1

⇒z2-z0=2

Now given, z0, z1 & z2 are collinear,

So, by concept of rotation we get,

z2-z0z1-z0=z2-z0z1-z0ei0

⇒z2-z0z1-z0=z2-z0z1-z0±1

⇒z2-z0z1-z0=±2

⇒z2=z0±2z1-z0

So, z2=2z1-z0=32+12i⇒z22=52

Or z2=3z0-2z1=-12+52i⇒z22=132

Hence, the smaller value will be, z22=52

Then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><munder><mo>∑</mo><mrow><mi>z</mi><mo>∈</mo><mi>S</mi></mrow></munder><mo>|</mo><mi>z</mi><msup><mo>|</mo><mn>2</mn></msup></math> is equal to
questions.solution:
Given,

z¯=iz2+Re(z¯)

Now, let z=x+iy, so z¯=x-iy

Now putting the value in, z¯=iz2+Re(z¯) we get,

z¯=iz2+Re(z¯)

⇒x-iy=ix2-y2+2ixy+x

⇒x-iy=ix2-y2+x-2xy

Now comparing real part we get,

⇒x=-2xy⇒x(2y+1)=0

⇒x=0,y=-12 .....1

And imaginary part we get,

-y=x2-y2+x .......2

Now taking Case (I) when x=0 in equation 2 we get,

⇒-y=-y2

⇒y2-y=0⇒y=0,1

So,  z=0, i

Now taking Case (II) when y=-12 in equation 2 we get,

⇒12=x2-14+x

⇒x2+x-34=0

⇒4x+4x-3=0

⇒(2x-1)(2x+3)=0

⇒x=12,-32

So, z=12-12i,-32-12i

Now finding, ∑|z|2=0+1+12+52=4

questions.q: The probability that a randomly chosen <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mo>×</mo><mn>2</mn></math> matrix with all the entries from the set of first <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>10</mn></math> primes, is singular, is equal to
questions.solution:
Let $\mathrm{A}=\left[\begin{array}{ll}\mathrm{a} & \mathrm{b} \\ \mathrm{c} & \mathrm{d}\end{array}\right]$ where $\mathrm{a}, \mathrm{b}, \mathrm{c}, \mathrm{d}$ are prime.
Total number of matrices A formed $=10^4$
Let $\mathrm{S}$ be a matrix from set of matrices $\mathrm{A}$ such that $\mathrm{S}$ is singular.
For singular matrix $|\mathrm{S}|=\mathrm{ad}-\mathrm{bc}=0 \Rightarrow \mathrm{ad}=\mathrm{bc}$
Number of singular matrices $=$ All entries are same $+$ only two prime number are used in matrix
then number of matrices with two prime numbers $=(10 \times 9) \times 2 !=180$
then number of matrices with one prime number $=10 \times 1=10$
So required probability $=\frac{10 \times 9 \times 2 !+10}{10^4}=\frac{19}{1000}=0.019$

questions.solution:
Given, A=mnpq,  A-dadjA=0

Here, d=|A|=mq-np

⇒A-dadjA=mnpq-dq-n-pm=0

⇒m-qdn1+dp1+dq-md=0

⇒m-qdq-md-np1+d2=0

⇒mq-m2d-q2d+mqd2-np1+2d+d2=0

⇒mq-m2d-q2d+mqd2-np-2npd-npd2=0

⇒mq-np+d2mq-np-dm2+q2+2np=0

⇒(mq-np)(1+d2)-d(m+q)2=0

⇒1+d2=d(m+q)2(mq-np)

⇒1+d2=(mq-np)(m+q)2(mq-np)    ∵ d=mq-np

⇒1+d2=m+q2

Hence, option A is correct.

questions.q: Let P be a square matrix such that P2=I-P. For α, β, γ, δ∈ℕ, if Pα+Pβ=γl-29P and Pα-Pβ=δl-13P, then α+β+γ-δ is equal to
questions.solution:
Given,

P be a square matrix such that P2=I-P,

Now solving P2=I-P we get,

⇒P4=I-PI-P

⇒P4=I+P2-2P

⇒P4=I+I-P-2P=2I-3P

Now solving, P4·P2=P6=2I-5P+3P2 

⇒P6=2I-5P+3I-P=5I-8P... i

Similarly P8=13I-21P ... ii

Now adding ii+i we get,

P8+P6=18I-29P

Now subtracting ii-i we get,

P8-P6=8I-13P

Now comparing with Pα-Pβ=δl-13P & Pα+Pβ=γl-29P we get,

α=8, β=6, γ=18, δ=8

Hence, α+β+γ-δ=8+6+18-8=24

 ⇒A=1001+015100=I+E

∴ An=I+En=I+nE+nC2E2⏝0+nC3E3⏟0+nC4E4⏟0...........

 ⇒An=1n5101

=C314-C33-C35-C36

=14×13×12×11!3×2×1×11!-1-5×4×3!3!×2×1-6×5×4×3!3×2×1×3!

364-1-10-20=333.

Hence, total number of triangles is 333.

We see that this is an infinite G.P. with common ratio P(A¯)×P(B¯)

Thus, probability that A wins =P(A)1-P(A¯)P(B¯)
=5361-3136·3036=3061

questions.q: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>B</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>C</mi></math> be three events such that the probability that exactly one of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>B</mi></math> occurs is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>(</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>-</mo><mi>k</mi><mo>)</mo><mo>,</mo></math> the probability that exactly one of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>B</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>C</mi></math> occurs is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>(</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn><mi>k</mi><mo>)</mo><mo>,</mo></math> the probability that exactly one of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>C</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi></math> occurs is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>(</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>-</mo><mi>k</mi><mo>)</mo></math> and the probability of all <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>B</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>C</mi></math> occur simultaneously is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>k</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>,</mo></math> where <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>0</mn><mo><</mo><mi>k</mi><mo><</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>.</mo></math> Then the probability that at least one of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>B</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>C</mi></math> occur is:
questions.solution:
Given:

P(A¯∩B)+P(A∩B¯)=1-k

P(A¯∩C)+P(A∩C¯)=1-k

P(B¯∩C)+P(B∩C¯)=1-2k

P(A∩B∩C)=k2

Now,

P(A)+P(B)-2P(A∩B)=1-k …i

P(B)+P(C)-2P(B∩C)=1-2k …ii

P(C)+P(A)-2P(A∩C)=1-k …iii

Adding i, ii & iii, we get

PA+PB+PC-PA∩B-PB∩C-PC∩A=-4k+32

Now,

PA∪B∪C=PA+PB+PC-PA∩B-PB∩C-PC∩A+PA∩B∩C

⇒PA∪B∪C=-4k+32+k2

⇒PA∪B∪C=2k2-4k+32

⇒PA∪B∪C=2(k-1)2+12

⇒PA∪B∪C=k-12+12

⇒P(A∪B∪C)>12

questions.q: Two squares are chosen at random on a chessboard (see figure). The probability that they have a side in common is :
questions.solution:
Total ways of choosing 2 squares =C264=32×63

Now, each corners has two neighbours, and each 24  squares across the outer edge of the chess board has 3 neighbours, and remaining 36 squares have four neighbours

Favorable ways =124(2)+24(3)+36(4)=12224=112.Since, every pair counted twice. 

Required probability =11232×63=118

questions.q: Four dice are thrown simultaneously and the numbers shown on these dice are recorded in <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mo>×</mo><mn>2</mn></math> matrices. The probability that such formed matrices have all different entries and are non-singular, is:
questions.solution:
Let, the outcomes of the four dice are a, b, c, d.

Then, we have the matrix A=abcd.

Also, A=abcd

⇒ A=ad-bc

Now, for each of the numbers a, b, c & d there are 6 possibilities, hence total cases=64.

For a non-singular matrix, A≠0

⇒ad-bc≠0

⇒ad≠bc

Also, a, b, c, d are all different numbers from the set {1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6}, thus we have total P46=6!6-4!

=6!2!=6×5×4×3×2!2!=360.

Now, for ad=bc

i 6×1=2×3, here we have two cases possible.

When ad=6×1 & bc=2×3, thus, we can choose a & d in P22=2 ways and the same we can do for b & c, and hence, we have total 2×2=4 ways.

Similarly, we can take  ad=2×3 & bc=6×1, and for this also we have total 4 ways.

Thus, we have total 4+4=8 ways.

ii 6×2=3×4, again here also, we have 8 cases possible.

Thus, the favourable cases =P46-16=360-16=344

And, hence the required probability=34464=3441296=43162.

questions.q: The number of real roots of the equation <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msqrt><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>4</mn><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>3</mn></msqrt><mo>+</mo><msqrt><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>9</mn></msqrt><mo>=</mo><msqrt><mn>4</mn><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>14</mn><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>6</mn></msqrt></math>, is:
questions.solution:
Given,

x2-4x+3+x2-9=4x2-14x+6

⇒x-3x-1+x-3x+3=x-34x-2

⇒x-3x-1+x-3x+3-x-34x-2=0

⇒x-3=0 or x-1+x+3-4x-2=0

So, x=3 is one solution and also x≥3

Now solving x-1+x+3-4x-2=0

⇒x-1+x+3=4x-2

Now squaring both side we get,

⇒x-1+x+3+2x-1x+3=4x-2

⇒2x-1x+3=2x-4

⇒x-1x+3=x-22

⇒x2+2x-3=x2-4x+4

⇒6x=7⇒x=76 which does not satisfy the given expression and x≥3

So, there is only one solution which is x=3

questions.solution:

tt-1+2=t-1+t-2
t2-t+2=t-1+t-2

Let, ft=t2-t+2 & gt=t-1+t-2

ft & gt has a solution at t=a.

questions.q: The equation <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>e</mi><mrow><mn>4</mn><mi>x</mi></mrow></msup><mo>+</mo><mn>8</mn><msup><mi>e</mi><mrow><mn>3</mn><mi>x</mi></mrow></msup><mo>+</mo><mn>13</mn><msup><mi>e</mi><mrow><mn>2</mn><mi>x</mi></mrow></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>8</mn><msup><mi>e</mi><mi>x</mi></msup><mo>+</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>∈</mo><mi>R</mi></math> has :
options: four solutions two of which are negative
two solutions and both are negative
two solutions and only one of them is negative
questions.solution:
Given,

e4x+8e3x+13e2x-8ex+1=0, x∈R

Now let ex=t we get,

t4+8t3+13t2-8t+1=0

Now divide complete equation by t2

⇒t2+1t2+8t-1t+13=0

⇒t-1t2+8t-1t+15=0

Now let t-1t=z we get,

⇒z2+8z+15=0

⇒z=-3,-5

So, t-1t=-3  or   t-1t=-5

⇒t2+3t-1=0  or   t2+5t-1=0

⇒t=-3±132,-5±292

So, ex=-3+132,-5+292=α,β (rejecting negative values as exponential is positive function)

And both -3+132 and -5+292∈0,1

So, x=lnα, lnβ are both negative,

Hence, there are two solution and both are negative.

questions.solution:
Let S=x+y+x2+xy+y2+x3+x2y+xy2+y3+.....

Multiply x-y both sides

⇒x-yS=x-y(x+y+x2+xy+y2+x3+x2y+xy2+y3+...)

⇒x-yS=x-yx+y+x-yx2+xy+y2+x-yx3+x2y+xy2+y3+.....

⇒x-yS=x2-y2+x3-y3+x4-y4+.....

⇒x-yS=x2+x3+x4+....-y2+y3+y4+.....

S=1x-yx21-x-y21-y=1x-yx2-x2y-y2+xy2(1-x)(1-y)

S=x+y-xy(1-x)(1-y)

⇒  ap-b=0,bp-c=0,cp-d=0

⇒  p=ba=cb=dc

questions.q: Let the mean and the variance of 5 observations x1,x2,x3,x4,x5 be 245 and 19425 respectively. If the mean and variance of the first 4 observation are 72 and a respectively, then 4a+x5 is equal to
questions.solution:
Mean x¯=∑xi5=245⇒∑xi=24   ...i

Variance σ2=∑xi25-2452=19425

⇒∑xi2=154   ...ii

Also given meanx1+x2+x3+x44=72⇒x1+x2+x3+x4=14

⇒x5=10 (from i)

and variance σ2=x12+x22+x32+x424-494=a

⇒x12+x22+x32+x42=4a+49

⇒x52=154-4a-49 (from ii)

⇒100=105-4a⇒4a=5

Hence 4a+x5=15

questions.q: The first of the two samples in a group has <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>100</mn></math> items with mean <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>15</mn></math> and standard deviation <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>3</mn><mo>.</mo></math> If the whole group has <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>250</mn></math> items with mean <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>15</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>6</mn></math> and standard deviation <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msqrt><mn>13</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>44</mn></msqrt><mo>,</mo></math> then the standard deviation of the second sample is:
questions.solution:
We have, number of terms in the first group n1=100 with their mean X1=15 and number of terms in whole group is n1+n2=250 and their mean X=15.6.

Variance of first groupV1x=9 and whole group is Varx=13.44.

Now, σ2=n1σ12+n2σ22n1+n2+n1n2n1+n22x¯1-x¯22

and n2=150,x¯2=16,V2(x)=σ2

Therefore, 13.44=100×9+150×σ22250+100×1502502×1

⇒σ22=16

⇒σ2=4
